examples.title: Kanban WIP Limit Calculation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate initial WIP limits based on historical sprint data for teams transitioning from Scrum
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how to derive WIP limits from historical data. The algorithm calculates average work items per sprint, applies a reduction factor to create conservative initial limits, and provides a formula teams can implement in spreadsheet tools or project management software.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of historical sprints analyzed

examples.title: Little's Law Flow Calculator
examples.description: Implementation of Little's Law for predicting lead time based on WIP and throughput measurements
examples.explanation: This code calculates any of the three flow metrics (Lead Time, WIP, Throughput) given the other two. It enables teams to plan capacity, predict delivery dates, and set appropriate WIP limits using mathematical optimization rather than arbitrary values.

examples.title: DSDM MoSCoW Prioritization Calculator
examples.description: Algorithm to validate MoSCoW distribution and ensure 60% maximum rule for Must-Have requirements
examples.explanation: This algorithm validates that requirements distribution follows DSDM guidelines. It checks that Must-Haves don't exceed 60%, calculates minimum viable delivery percentage, and identifies when scope negotiation is needed. Useful for project managers ensuring timebox discipline.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of requirements
